top_findings.assumption: Timestamp-to-date logic produces stable reporting buckets across this change.
top_findings.trigger: A related timestamp/timezone/date-bucketing change is present in the PR.
top_findings.why_it_matters: Events near day boundaries can silently move between reporting days, changing dashboards without query failures.
top_findings.evidence_excerpt: select date(event_ts) as reporting_day, sum(amount_usd) as revenue_usd, count(*) as event_count from analytics.stg_events group by 1
top_findings.recommended_check: Run a before/after bucket comparison by day and timezone for the affected timestamp over a recent representative window, especially midnight-boundary records.

pattern_detail.boundary_risk: records near midnight can move between reporting buckets

business_impact.summary: This finding reaches board critical assets: Executive Revenue Dashboard.

control_coverage.experimental_note: Cybersec/DevOps-inspired control coverage: advisory-only, used to improve calibration without blocking.

assumption_diff.old_assumption: Timestamp-to-date bucketing remained stable across reporting boundaries.
assumption_diff.new_assumption: Timestamp/timezone/date-bucketing semantics may move records across reporting boundaries.
assumption_diff.drift_summary: Daily/hourly bucket meaning may differ before vs after this PR.
assumption_diff.evidence_excerpt: select date(event_ts) as reporting_day, sum(amount_usd) as revenue_usd, count(*) as event_count from analytics.stg_events group by 1
assumption_diff.advisory_note: Assumption diff is static PR-context evidence, not behavioral replay.
